place: Butler borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Butler was named after Richard Butler, whose expansion of the local rubber mill sparked the town’s rapid growth in the late 1800s.
The former New York Susquehanna and Western Railroad station now houses the Butler Museum, which displays unique artifacts linked to the Statue of Liberty and its designer, Frederic Bartholdi.
The Butler Rubber Mill once held the title of the world’s largest hard rubber factory, drawing workers from across the region.
Gifford Street honors American landscape painter Sanford R. Gifford, whose works captured the New Jersey countryside.
Every element of Butler’s coat of arms symbolizes a chapter of the town’s history, from industry to community life.
